title: On the Limits of Self-Improving in Large Language Models: The Singularity Is Not Near Without Symbolic Model Synthesis
summary: This paper argues that large language models (LLMs) cannot achieve superhuman intelligence through self-improvement alone, as they lack the ability to synthesize new symbolic models. It contends that true AI advancement requires integrating symbolic reasoning with LLMs, challenging the notion that scaling or self-play will lead to a singularity.
